insight:
Yesterday was a solid active recovery day with 9,653 steps and no formal training sessions, which is smart positioning just 6 days out from Chester. Your body burned 2,471 calories naturally, showing good metabolic activity. However, that 5.8 hours of sleep is concerning this close to race day - your body needs more recovery time to be fresh for the half marathon.

Your HRV at 95ms sits right in your baseline sweet spot (93-115ms), and that 49 bpm resting heart rate shows excellent cardiovascular fitness. With stress low at 34/100, your recovery markers are actually looking good despite the short sleep. This suggests you can proceed with today's planned session without modification.

Focus on sleep debt recovery tonight - aim for 8+ hours to compensate for yesterday's shortfall. Your body is primed for performance, but consistent quality sleep over these final days will make the difference between feeling strong or sluggish at the start line.
